fields.Name: SharePoint
fields.Description: Microsoft SharePoint Online connector via Microsoft Graph API v1.0. Syncs Sites, Drives, DriveItems (files/folders), Lists, ListItems, ContentTypes, Columns, Permissions, and Subscriptions. Authenticates via OAuth2 Client Credentials (Azure app registr...

Configuration.AuthFlowNote: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials grant (RFC 6749 §4.4). The service application uses client_id + client_secret (or client_assertion for certificate-based) to obtain a Bearer token from the tenant-specific token endpoint without user interaction. No refresh_token is issued for client_credentials — re-acquire by submitting a new token request when the current token is within expiry. Source: Microsoft Entra v2-oauth2-client-creds-grant-flow docs.
Configuration.AuthHeaderPatternSource: Microsoft Graph auth-concepts page: the app attaches the access token as a Bearer token to the Authorization header. Example: Authorization: Bearer <token_value>. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/auth/auth-concepts
Configuration.TokenEndpointNote: tenant is the Azure AD tenant ID (GUID) or domain name — NEVER a constant baked into the connector. Mechanism: POST with Content-Type application/x-www-form-urlencoded body: client_id={clientId}&client_secret={clientSecret}&grant_type=client_credentials&scope=https%3A%2F%2Fgraph.microsoft.com%2F.default. The /.default scope requests all application permissions pre-consented for the app registration. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/v2-oauth2-client-creds-grant-flow
Configuration.TokenScopeNote: The /.default scope suffix requests all application permissions pre-consented for the registered app. Vendor states: the value tells the Microsoft identity platform that of all the direct application permissions configured for your app, the endpoint should issue a token for the ones associated with the resource you want to use. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/v2-oauth2-client-creds-grant-flow
Configuration.TokenRefreshStrategyNote: Client Credentials tokens are short-lived (~3599 seconds, i.e. ~1 hour, as returned in expires_in). No refresh_token is issued. Re-acquire by submitting a new token request. Source: expires_in: 3599 in example token response at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/v2-oauth2-client-creds-grant-flow
Configuration.APIVersioningStrategyNote: Version is embedded in the URL path: https://graph.microsoft.com/{version}/{resource}. Two versions supported: v1.0 (GA, use in production) and beta (preview, breaking changes possible). Connector targets v1.0 only. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/use-the-api

PaginationDefaults.cursorNote: When more pages exist, Microsoft Graph returns an @odata.nextLink property containing the absolute URL to the next page. The client follows this URL verbatim — never reconstruct it from base URL + params. Pagination terminates when @odata.nextLink is absent from the response. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/paging
PaginationDefaults.pageSizeParamNote: Clients may request a page size via the $top OData query parameter. The service may return fewer items than requested and may ignore $top for certain resources. Never rely on $top being honored exactly — always follow @odata.nextLink.

IncrementalSyncCapability.deltaLinkNote: Microsoft Graph delta query returns @odata.deltaLink at the end of each full enumeration round. Persist this token durably. On subsequent syncs, follow the deltaLink URL to receive only changed/deleted items since the previous sync. The token is opaque — do not parse or reconstruct it. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/delta-query-overview
IncrementalSyncCapability.watermarkMechanismNote: IncrementalWatermarkField is the @odata.deltaLink token (opaque, absolute URL). Stored and used verbatim. Full resync is required when the token becomes invalid (HTTP 410 Gone + Location header containing empty $deltatoken, or syncStateNotFound error). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/delta-query-overview
IncrementalSyncCapability.invalidTokenBehavior: On HTTP 410 Gone or syncStateNotFound error, discard the deltaLink and restart from a full enumeration (issue the initial delta query with no deltatoken). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/delta-query-overview — Synchronization reset section.
IncrementalSyncCapability.paginationDuringDelta: During a delta sync round, @odata.nextLink is returned for intermediate pages (not yet done); @odata.deltaLink is returned on the final page (round complete). A page cannot contain both. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/delta-query-overview

Configuration.WebhooksNote: SharePoint webhooks (REST) allow subscriptions on list items. Subscription lifecycle managed via POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE to subscription endpoints. Notification delivery uses a validation-token handshake (GET with ?validationtoken={randomString} — respond with the token as plain text). Notification payload does not include resource data — client must poll for changes. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/dev/apis/webhooks/overview-sharepoint-webhooks
Configuration.WebhooksSignatureNote: No HMAC or shared-secret signature algorithm is documented for SharePoint REST webhooks. Authentication is via the subscription validation handshake only — the receiver must echo back the validationtoken. Marking as null (not documented). Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sharepoint/dev/apis/webhooks/overview-sharepoint-webhooks
Configuration.BulkOperationsNote: Microsoft Graph JSON batching allows up to 20 individual requests to be combined into a single HTTP POST to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/$batch. Each subrequest is processed independently. A 200 batch response does not indicate individual-request success — each subrequest has its own status code. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/json-batching

universalPK.notes: Microsoft Graph resources use id as the universal primary-key field name across sites, lists, list items, drives, drive items, users, groups, and all SharePoint/OneDrive objects. The id is a system-assigned opaque GUID-based string. Source: Graph API reference — every resource type exposes id as its identifier. Runtime D4 SoftPKClassifier will validate per-IO. Note: the Sites object uses a composite id (hostname,siteCollectionGuid,webGuid) that must be treated as an opaque string.

WriteCapability.notes: Microsoft Graph SharePoint surface supports full CRUD: create/read/update/delete for Lists, ListItems, Sites (partial — create site is admin-only), Drives (read-only enumeration), DriveItems (full CRUD including upload), ContentTypes, Columns, Permissions, Subscriptions. ListItems support PATCH for field updates. DriveItems support DELETE. Source: Microsoft Graph SharePoint API reference at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/sharepoint

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNote: Microsoft Graph uses ETag / @odata.etag on resources that support it (DriveItems, ListItems). Clients may send If-Match headers on PATCH/DELETE to enforce optimistic concurrency. On a conflict the API returns HTTP 412 Precondition Failed. Source: OData protocol used by Graph; Graph DriveItem documentation notes ETag support on file items.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNote: DriveItem delete sends items to the Recycle Bin (soft delete). The item is not immediately permanently removed — it can be restored. Delta query surfaces deleted items with a deleted facet ({deleted: {state: deleted}}). ListItem delete via Graph is a hard delete (item removed immediately, no recycle bin recovery via Graph). Source: Microsoft Graph DriveItem delta and Recycle Bin documentation.
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionReasoning: Read-only (Pull) by default per policy. Write-path (bidirectional) requires sandbox validation before enabling against a client system. ETag support exists for DriveItems/ListItems but not all resources.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionReasoning: ETag / @odata.etag is available on DriveItems and supports optimistic concurrency. When etag concurrency control is enabled, MostRecent conflict resolution is viable. For resources without etag support, fall back to DestWins.

error.code: string — machine-readable error code (e.g. badRequest, unauthorized, forbidden, notFound, tooManyRequests, internalServerError)

innererror.code: string — nested error code for sub-errors

error.details: array — optional list of sub-error objects

RateLimitPolicy.policyNote: Microsoft Graph returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests when throttled. The response includes a Retry-After header specifying the number of seconds to wait before retrying. Do not retry before the Retry-After interval elapses. If a retry returns 429 again, continue respecting the Retry-After delay until success. Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/throttling

Configuration.CustomObjectMarkerPatternNote: SharePoint does not have a naming convention for custom objects in the Graph API. Custom lists are created at runtime per site and discoverable via GET /sites/{siteId}/lists. There is no prefix/suffix pattern that identifies them as custom vs system lists.

CustomFieldMarkerPattern.notes: Custom list columns (site columns, list columns) are discoverable via GET /sites/{siteId}/columns and /sites/{siteId}/lists/{listId}/columns. System columns are identified by isSealed=true; custom (user-defined) columns have isSealed=false. The Graph column resource exposes columnGroup and description to further classify columns.

OutOfScopeObjectFamilies.reason: SharePoint Framework UI/web-parts/command-sets — UI extensibility, not a data-sync surface.
SharePoint REST/CSOM-only operations — used only where Graph lacks coverage, isolated behind an adapter; not the default sync surface.
Microsoft Search — discovery aid, never a source of truth for sync (read by stable IDs via delta/query).

Configuration.BiDirectionalPreconditions: Before enabling bidirectional sync: (1) Validate in a SharePoint sandbox/test tenant that write operations produce expected results for the target object family. (2) DriveItem deletes move to Recycle Bin — orphan removal does not permanently delete immediately. (3) ListItem deletes via Graph are immediate and irreversible — validate carefully. (4) ETag concurrency control exists on DriveItems; use If-Match on PATCH/DELETE to prevent overwrites. (5) Use MostRecent conflict resolution for ETag-enabled resources; DestWins for others. (6) Validate Application permissions (not Delegated) are sufficient for the target write operations in the app registration.

fields.Name: Site
fields.Description: Microsoft Graph v1.0 SharePoint microsoft.graph.site record. Synced via /sites. Access path: sites.

fields.Name: id
fields.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only. (PK — universal Microsoft Graph `id` convention; every Graph resource exposes id as its opaque system identifier.)

fields.Name: createdBy
fields.Description: (embedded microsoft.graph.identitySet facet; stored as JSON)

fields.Name: createdDateTime
fields.Description: Date and time of item creation. Read-only.

fields.Name: description
fields.Description: Provides a user-visible description of the item. Optional.

fields.Name: eTag
fields.Description: ETag for the item. Read-only.

fields.Name: lastModifiedBy
fields.Description: (embedded microsoft.graph.identitySet facet; stored as JSON)

fields.Name: lastModifiedDateTime
fields.Description: Date and time the item was last modified. Read-only.

fields.Name: name
fields.Description: The name of the item. Read-write.

fields.Name: parentReference
fields.Description: (embedded microsoft.graph.itemReference facet; stored as JSON)

fields.Name: webUrl
fields.Description: URL that either displays the resource in the browser (for Office file formats), or is a direct link to the file (for other formats). Read-only.

fields.Name: createdByUser
fields.Description: (embedded microsoft.graph.user facet; stored as JSON)

fields.Name: lastModifiedByUser
fields.Description: (embedded microsoft.graph.user facet; stored as JSON)

fields.Name: displayName
fields.Description: The full title for the site. Read-only.

fields.Description: (embedded microsoft.graph.publicError facet; stored as JSON)

fields.Name: isPersonalSite
fields.Description: Identifies whether the site is personal or not. Read-only.

fields.Name: Subscription
fields.Description: Microsoft Graph v1.0 SharePoint microsoft.graph.subscription record. Synced via /subscriptions. Access path: subscriptions.

fields.Name: id
fields.Description: The unique identifier for an entity. Read-only. (PK — universal Microsoft Graph `id` convention; every Graph resource exposes id as its opaque system identifier.)

fields.Name: applicationId
fields.Description: Optional. Identifier of the application used to create the subscription. Read-only.

fields.Name: changeType
fields.Description: Required. Indicates the type of change in the subscribed resource that raises a change notification. The supported values are: created, updated, deleted. Multiple values can be combined using a comma-separated list. Note:  Drive root item and list change notifications support only the updated changeType. User and group change notifications support updated and deleted changeType. Use updated to receive notifications when user or group is created, updated, or soft deleted. Use deleted to receive notifications when user or group is permanently deleted.

fields.Name: clientState
fields.Description: Optional. Specifies the value of the clientState property sent by the service in each change notification. The maximum length is 128 characters. The client can check that the change notification came from the service by comparing the value of the clientState property sent with the subscription with the value of the clientState property received with each change notification.

fields.Name: creatorId
fields.Description: Optional. Identifier of the user or service principal that created the subscription. If the app used delegated permissions to create the subscription, this field contains the ID of the signed-in user the app called on behalf of. If the app used application permissions, this field contains the ID of the service principal corresponding to the app. Read-only.

fields.Name: encryptionCertificate
fields.Description: Optional. A base64-encoded representation of a certificate with a public key used to encrypt resource data in change notifications. Optional but required when includeResourceData is true.

fields.Name: encryptionCertificateId
fields.Description: Optional. A custom app-provided identifier to help identify the certificate needed to decrypt resource data.

fields.Name: expirationDateTime
fields.Description: Required. Specifies the date and time when the webhook subscription expires. The time is in UTC, and can be an amount of time from subscription creation that varies for the resource subscribed to. Any value under 45 minutes after the time of the request is automatically set to 45 minutes after the request time. For the maximum supported subscription length of time, see Subscription lifetime.

fields.Name: includeResourceData
fields.Description: Optional. When set to true, change notifications include resource data (such as content of a chat message).

fields.Name: latestSupportedTlsVersion
fields.Description: Optional. Specifies the latest version of Transport Layer Security (TLS) that the notification endpoint, specified by notificationUrl, supports. The possible values are: v10, v11, v12, v13. For subscribers whose notification endpoint supports a version lower than the currently recommended version (TLS 1.2), specifying this property by a set timeline allows them to temporarily use their deprecated version of TLS before completing their upgrade to TLS 1.2. For these subscribers, not setting this property per the timeline would result in subscription operations failing. For subscribers whose notification endpoint already supports TLS 1.2, setting this property is optional. In such cases, Microsoft Graph defaults the property to v1_2.

fields.Name: lifecycleNotificationUrl
fields.Description: Required for Teams resources if  the expirationDateTime value is more than 1 hour from now; optional otherwise. The URL of the endpoint that receives lifecycle notifications, including subscriptionRemoved, reauthorizationRequired, and missed notifications. This URL must make use of the HTTPS protocol. For more information, see Reduce missing subscriptions and change notifications.

fields.Name: notificationQueryOptions
fields.Description: Optional. OData query options for specifying value for the targeting resource. Clients receive notifications when resource reaches the state matching the query options provided here. With this new property in the subscription creation payload along with all existing properties, Webhooks deliver notifications whenever a resource reaches the desired state mentioned in the notificationQueryOptions property. For example, when the print job is completed or when a print job resource isFetchable property value becomes true etc.  Supported only for Universal Print Service. For more information, see Subscribe to change notifications from cloud printing APIs using Microsoft Graph.

fields.Name: notificationUrl
fields.Description: Required. The URL of the endpoint that receives the change notifications. This URL must make use of the HTTPS protocol. Any query string parameter included in the notificationUrl property is included in the HTTP POST request when Microsoft Graph sends the change notifications.

fields.Name: notificationUrlAppId
fields.Description: Optional. The app ID that the subscription service can use to generate the validation token. The value allows the client to validate the authenticity of the notification received.

fields.Name: resource
fields.Description: Required. Specifies the resource that is monitored for changes. Don't include the base URL (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/). See the possible resource path values for each supported resource.

fields.Description: Specifies whether the label is applied explicitly on the item. True indicates that the label is applied explicitly; otherwise, the label is inherited from its parent. Read-only.

fields.Name: labelAppliedBy

fields.Name: labelAppliedDateTime
fields.Description: The date and time when the label was applied on the item. The timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format and is always in UTC. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2014 is 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z. Read-only.
